evals.prompt: I have a customer support bot that sometimes mentions competitor products and occasionally writes responses over 200 words. I need to enforce these rules strictly.
evals.expected_output: A DSPy module wrapped with dspy.Refine using a reward function that checks word count and competitor mentions, with graduated scoring that penalizes each violation.

assertions.name: uses_refine_or_bestn
assertions.description: Uses dspy.Refine or dspy.BestOfN (not deprecated dspy.Assert) for constraint enforcement

assertions.name: has_reward_function
assertions.description: Defines a reward function with (args, pred) signature returning a float

assertions.name: checks_word_count_in_reward
assertions.description: Reward function checks word count and penalizes violations

assertions.name: provider_agnostic
assertions.description: No hardcoded LM provider without alternative comment

evals.prompt: My quiz generator sometimes produces questions where the correct answer is not in the options list, and sometimes options are duplicated. How do I enforce this?
evals.expected_output: A DSPy module combining Pydantic for structural validation with dspy.Refine and a reward function for logic constraints (correct_answer in options, unique options).

assertions.name: pydantic_plus_refine
assertions.description: Uses both Pydantic model for structure and dspy.Refine with reward function for logic constraints

assertions.name: checks_answer_in_options
assertions.description: Reward function checks that correct_answer is one of the options

assertions.name: checks_unique_options
assertions.description: Reward function checks all options are unique

assertions.name: has_reward_function
assertions.description: Defines a reward function with (args, pred) signature returning a float

evals.prompt: I want my AI to follow brand voice guidelines - no slang, end with a sign-off, keep it professional. But I don't want it to hard-fail on the sign-off if it's otherwise good.
evals.expected_output: A DSPy module using dspy.Refine with a graduated reward function where hard rules (no slang) have heavy penalties and soft preferences (sign-off) have light penalties.

assertions.name: graduated_reward
assertions.description: Reward function uses graduated scoring with different weights for hard vs soft rules

assertions.name: hard_rules_heavy_penalty
assertions.description: Hard constraints like no slang have large score penalties (e.g., -0.3)

assertions.name: soft_rules_light_penalty
assertions.description: Soft preferences like sign-off have small score penalties (e.g., -0.1)

assertions.name: uses_refine_or_bestn
assertions.description: Uses dspy.Refine or dspy.BestOfN (not deprecated dspy.Assert/Suggest)
